step_by_step_thinking:
One unit of packed red blood cells (PRBCs) typically increases hematocrit (HCT) by approximately:
The key piece of information here comes directly from understanding how much one unit of PRBC affects Hematocrit (HCT).
\boxed{C} Based on typical medical literature and clinical practice guidelines, one unit of packed red blood cells usually raises the hematocrit level by about 3-5%. Therefore, the best fit among the given choices is C: 3-5%.
Given the focus on the effect of PRBC units on HCT rather than HCV, and considering standard dosages and responses seen in clinical settings, option C accurately captures the expected range. The exact percentage might vary slightly depending on individual physiology, but generally, 3-5% aligns well with common observations. Hence, I choose
